back_translation: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:
"Send for yourself men, and let them spy out the land of Canaan which I am giving to the sons of Israel; one man, one man for each tribe of his fathers you shall send, each a leader among them."
And Moses sent them from the wilderness of Paran according to the word of the LORD; all of them were men, heads of the sons of Israel they were.
And these were their names: for the tribe of Reuben, Shammua son of Zaccur.
For the tribe of Simeon, Shaphat son of Hori.
For the tribe of Judah, Caleb son of Jephunneh.
For the tribe of Issachar, Igal son of Joseph.
For the tribe of Ephraim, Hoshea son of Nun.
For the tribe of Benjamin, Palti son of Raphu.
For the tribe of Zebulun, Gaddiel son of Sodi.
For the tribe of Joseph, for the tribe of Manasseh, Gaddi son of Susi.
For the tribe of Dan, Ammiel son of Gemalli.
For the tribe of Asher, Sethur son of Michael.
For the tribe of Naphtali, Nahbi son of Vophsi.
And for the tribe of Gad, Geuel son of Machi.
These were the names of the men whom Moses sent to spy out the land. And Moses called Hoshea son of Nun, Joshua.
And Moses sent them to spy out the land of Canaan, and said to them: 'Go up this way through the Negev, and you shall go up to the hill country.'
And you shall see the land — what it is — and the people that dwell in it, whether strong or weak, whether few or many.
And what the land is in which they dwell, whether it is good or bad; and what the cities are in which they dwell, whether in camps or in fortifications.

And what the land is — whether it is fertile or lean, whether there are trees in it or not. And be courageous, and you shall take from the fruit of the land. And the days were the days of the firstfruits of grapes.
And they went up and spied out the land from the wilderness of Zin as far as Rehob, toward Lebo-hamath.
And they went up through the Negev, and he came as far as Hebron, and there were Ahiman, Sheshai, and Talmai, the descendants of Anak. And Hebron was built seven years before Zoan in Egypt.
And they came to the Valley of Eshcol, and they cut from there a branch and one cluster of grapes, and carried it on a pole between two; and some of the pomegranates and some of the figs.
To that place he called the Valley of Eshcol, because of the cluster which the sons of Israel cut from there.
And they returned from spying out the land at the end of forty days.

And they went and came to Moses and to Aaron and to all the congregation of the sons of Israel, to the wilderness of Paran, to Kadesh; and they brought back word to them and to all the congregation, and showed them the fruit of the land.
And they told him and said: 'We came to the land where you sent us, and indeed it is flowing with milk and honey, and this is its fruit.'
Nevertheless, the people that dwell in the land are strong, and the cities are very greatly fortified; and we also saw the descendants of Anak there.
Amalek dwells in the land of the Negev, and the Hittite, the Jebusite, and the Amorite dwell in the hill country, and the Canaanite dwells by the sea and along the Jordan.
And Caleb quieted the people before Moses, and said: 'Going up, we shall go up, and we shall possess it, for being able, we are able to do it.'
But the men who went up with him said: 'We are not able to go up against the people, for they are stronger than we.'

And they brought out a bad report of the land which they had spied out, to the sons of Israel, saying: 'The land which we crossed through to spy it out, it is a land that devours its inhabitants; and all the people whom we saw' —
'and there we saw the Nephilim — the descendants of Anak from the Nephilim — and we were in our own eyes as grasshoppers, and so we were in their eyes.'
